suggested_fixes: For the preferred optimized path, replace ATen matmul calls with three cuBLAS SGEMMs using the required row-major/column-major parameter patterns.
Avoid explicit B.T transpose materialization by using cuBLAS transpose flags.
Prefer checking inputs are already contiguous instead of silently copying them if targeting the 3_sgemm-style implementation.
Add a TORCH_CHECK that d is between 3584 and 4608 if strict compliance with the stated runtime range is required.
Add same-device validation for W, X, A, and B.
warnings: Candidate is an ATen reference implementation and uses at::matmul for the core computation; this is not a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM implementation.
The implementation materializes B.transpose(0, 1).contiguous(), creating an explicit transpose copy that a cuBLAS implementation could avoid with operation flags.
Inputs are copied with .contiguous(), which is safe but may add significant overhead and is not preferred for the 3_sgemm-style path.
No explicit check that d is within [3584, 4608]; the code supports dynamic square d generally, but does not enforce the specified runtime range.
No explicit check that all CUDA tensors are on the same device; ATen operations will likely raise an error if devices differ, but this is not validated up front.
